channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sanef launches independent inquiry into allegations against Makhudu Sefara
item.description: The South African National Editors’ Forum (Sanef) has initiated an independent legal inquiry following revelations by the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) suggesting that Sunday Times editor and forme...

item.title: Antoinette Lattouf reflects on resilience and advocacy after ABC legal case
item.description: Antoinette Lattouf, a journalist of Lebanese heritage, has spoken openly about the toll of her legal battle with the ABC following her termination after sharing a Human Rights Watch post on Gaza. Whil...

item.title: Gachagua Says Kenyan Workers Struggling with Low Pay and Rising Costs This Labour Day
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has expressed deep concerns about the plight of Kenyan workers in his Labour Day address, arguing that they have little reason to celebrate. He pointed out tha...

item.title: Landstuhl community reacts to US troop reduction plans
item.description: The town of Landstuhl in south-west Germany has a longstanding connection with American soldiers, dating back to the arrival of General George Patton’s Third Army in 1945. This unique US-German identi...

item.title: Trump Faces Growing Challenges as Iran Conflict Drags On
item.description: After more than two months of conflict between the U.S. and Iran, there is no clear resolution in sight, and President Trump faces rising political, economic, and strategic pressures. The war has not ...

item.title: Callum Hudson-Odoi fined for failing to tax McLaren 765LT
item.description: Nottingham Forest winger Callum Hudson-Odoi has been ordered by a court to pay over £1,100 after being convicted for failing to tax his McLaren 765LT. The 25-year-old Premier League footballer became ...

item.title: AFL Round Highlights: Carlton struggles, St Kilda dominates, Tigers and Cats secure wins
item.description: The 2026 AFL round has delivered mixed fortunes for several clubs. Carlton suffered another heavy defeat, losing to St Kilda by 39 points at Marvel Stadium, extending their poor form to six consecutiv...

item.title: U.S. Plans to Reduce Troop Presence in Germany Amid Tensions Over Iran War
item.description: The United States has announced plans to withdraw approximately 5,000 troops from Germany over the next six to twelve months, representing 14% of the 36,000 U.S. service members stationed there. The m...

item.title: Montreal Comes Alive with Spring and Hockey Playoffs
item.description: As Montreal transitions from a long winter to spring, the city’s mood is notably influenced by both the warming weather and the excitement surrounding the hockey playoffs. Gabrielle Drolet, in her wee...

item.title: Bollywood actors making their mark in Hollywood: breaking stereotypes and achieving global recognition
item.description: Bollywood actors are increasingly making waves in Hollywood, moving beyond the stereotypical roles that earlier Indian actors were often confined to, such as taxi drivers or comic side characters. Pio...

item.title: Sergey Brin expresses political views at Marin County gathering
item.description: At a recent holiday party hosted by a prominent cryptocurrency figure in Marin County, Google co-founder Sergey Brin took the opportunity to discuss political matters with California Governor Gavin Ne...

item.title: Kenya Fuel Prices Remain High Despite Fluctuating Global Oil Costs
item.description: In Kenya, the discussion on fuel prices remains heated as the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (Epra) adjusted pump prices in mid-April 2026, citing high global oil costs amid international d...

item.title: Princess Charlotte celebrates 11th birthday with new family video
item.description: Kensington Palace has shared a new video to celebrate Princess Charlotte’s 11th birthday. Charlotte, the only daughter of the Prince and Princess of Wales and their middle child, is shown enjoying a d...

item.title: Notable Indian Nonfiction Books to Read in May, Including Romila Thapar’s Memoir
item.description: This article presents a curated list of noteworthy Indian nonfiction books for May. Leading the list is historian Romila Thapar’s memoir, 'Just Being,' which offers an introspective journey through he...

item.title: FG to invest ₦250bn in 2026 to tackle student accommodation shortage nationwide
item.description: Federal Government don announce say dem go spend about ₦250 billion for 2026 to build new student hostels across universities and polytechnics for Nigeria. Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, talk th...

item.title: Canberra sport wrap: Women's rugby league sideline scuffle, Magpies return to improved home ground
item.description: A dramatic Saturday of Canberra sport saw a sideline fight break out in the women's Katrina Fanning Shield rugby league match between the Yass Magpies and Woden Valley Rams, after a hard tackle sparke...

item.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations Amid Rising Fuel Costs Due to Iran War
item.description: Spirit Airlines, a US-based budget carrier, has shut down after failing to secure support from creditors for a government bailout. The company, which once accounted for 5% of US flights, was unable to...

item.title: Real Madrid Defender Carvajal Out With Toe Injury Ahead of Clasico
item.description: Real Madrid don suffer small wahala as their veteran right-back, Dani Carvajal, don fracture him toe and e go miss the big Clasico match against Barcelona wey go happen next Sunday, May 10. E fit be h...

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations amid soaring jet fuel prices linked to Iran conflict
item.description: US discount carrier Spirit Airlines has officially ceased all operations on Saturday, becoming the first major casualty in the aviation industry due to the ongoing Iran war. The airline, already in a ...

item.title: AMSONS Group and Kenya partner to expand maternal healthcare with 10 new hospitals
item.description: Kenya has entered into a major agreement with Tanzania’s AMSONS Group to build 10 modern maternal and child health hospitals across nine counties, in a project valued at Tsh90.75 billion (about Ksh4.5...
